domain: Velvet worm (Onychophora) projectile slime jet — adhesive liquid that hardens on impact, immobilizing prey at distance via downstream entanglement.
specific_mechanism: Oscillating-papilla syringe ejects liquid proteinaceous slime at 3-5 m/s in 30-60 Hz oscillation; on contact, mechanical agitation triggers a rapid liquid-to-disordered-fiber-web phase transition that adhesively immobilizes the target at distance.
llm_application: SLIME-NET: on adversarial-token detection at position p, the defense controller PROJECTS a precomputed adhesive binding-vector to a downstream position p+k where the adversarial signal is predicted to propagate. The binding vector is in liquid (low-rank, soft) form during projection but undergoes a two-phase trigger: when adversarial-feature similarity exceeds threshold at p+k, the binding vector hardens into a stop-state matrix locking the attention pattern at p+k. Result: downstream entanglement rather than at-source ablation.
novelty_claim: AutoDefense, ProAct, G-Guard, stinging-nettle-style bundled discharge all fire at source on trigger. Token ablation/masking removes at source. Activation steering shifts at source. SLIME-NET uniquely projects defense to a PREDICTED downstream location and uses a TWO-PHASE liquid-to-solid hardening triggered by adversarial-feature arrival.
